Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Input A textbased pseudoBPMN representation of a business process Customer Request Handling Task Provide a detailed explanation of the underlying business process Specific Requirements Explain the purpose of each step Explain the meaning of the gateways Explain path convergence and looping Clarify differences between Standard vs Custom requests Clarify conditions for approvals and rework

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Deconstruct the PseudoBPMN Start Decision 1 XOR Check Request Type Path A Standard Validation Parallel Checks Credit Inventory Calculate Delivery Date Path B Custom Feasibility Analysis Feasibility Check Yes No Rejection End Yes Prepare Custom Quotation Convergence Point After Standard Path Task D or Custom Path Task E1 Decision 2 XOR Is Approval Needed Path C Yes Obtain Approval Approval Granted Yes No If No Re evaluate Loop back to E1 Custom or D Standard Path D No Approval Needed Generate Final Invoice Convergence After Approval Path Task G or No Approval Path Task G Final Step Send Confirmation End

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Drafting the Explanation Section by Section Introduction Briefly state what the process is Order Fulfillment Order Processing Phase 1 Intake Routing Task A Intake Gateway 1 XOR Standard vs Custom Phase 2 Divergent Processing Standard Validation Parallel Checks Credit Inventory Delivery Calc explain why parallel efficiency Custom Feasibility Analysis critical filter If not feasible stop Rejection If feasible Quote Phase 3 Convergence Approval Logic Both paths meet before Approval Gateway Gateway 2 XOR Approval Threshold Approval Path Manager sign off Rejection Path If manager says no rework loop Crucial detail loop back to E1 Custom or D Standard explain nuance reevaluation No Approval Path Skip to Invoice Phase 4 Finalization Task G Invoice Generation common Task I Customer Notification End

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Addressing Specific Questions in the Prompt Purpose of each step Map tasks to business value Credit Check Risk Management Meaning of gateways Explain XOR Exclusive vs AND Parallel Join Split Convergence Looping Explain how paths merge specific loop condition Approval Denied Standard vs Custom Contrast validation vs feasibility analysis Approval Rework Define trigger Manager Approval Needed Yes consequence Loop if No

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Refining and Polishing Tone Professional analytical clear Formatting Use bolding lists clear headings Correction Verification Wait check the loop diagram says Loop back to Task E1 Custom or Task D Standard implies conditions might change or quote needs adjustment need to make sure clear Check Convergence diagram shows After Standard or Custom Path Tasks Completed leading to Approval Gateway means Task D and Task E1 convergence points before Approval Gateway Check Task G appears in two places Approval Yes and No Approval merge point Visual Aid text description sufficient

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review against constraints Did I explain the XOR vs AND Yes Did I explain Standard vs Custom Yes Did I explain the loop Yes Is it detailed Yes

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Constructing the Output SelfCorrection during drafting Initial thought Just list steps Correction No group them logically Intake Processing Approval Fulfillment Emphasis Highlight risk management aspect Credit Check feasibility aspect Custom Loop Logic Be very specific about what happens if approval denied re evaluate

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Final Polish Check for clarity and flow The resulting output matches the provided good response
